resposta:
solution: 
```
class Solution:
    def findUnsortedSubarray(self, nums: List[int]) -> int:
        sorted_nums = sorted(nums)
        start, end = -1, -1
        for i in range(len(nums)):
            if nums[i] != sorted_nums[i]:
                if start == -1:
                    start = i
                end = i
        return 0 if start == -1 else end - start + 1
```

efficiency: HIGH

time complexity: O(n log n)

space complexity: O(n)

energy implications: LOW

explanation: This solution sorts the input array and then iterates through the array to find the first and last indices where the original array differs from the sorted array. The length of the subarray is then calculated as the difference between these two indices plus one.
